title: Applications of Pose Estimation
concept: Pose estimation powers diverse applications, including physical therapy, athletic training, action recognition, and virtual try-ons.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main benefit of Part Affinity Fields?

options.text: They provide direction vectors to connect joints to the correct skeleton.
They classify whether a person is running or sitting.
They speed up the camera frame decoding.

exercises.explain: Correct! PAFs encode the direction and affinity of limb segments, preventing joint scrambling when people overlap.

cards.title: Democratizing motion capture
cards.body: Motion capture used to be a luxury reserved for Hollywood film studios. Today, pose estimation brings this capability to everyone. By running keypoint tracking on a standard smartphone, a doctor can assess a patient's joint recovery, or a coach can analyze a runner's stride instantly.

cards.title: Real-World Use Cases
cards.body:
- Physical Therapy: Tracking joint angles to monitor range of motion during exercises.
- Action Recognition: Classifying movements (e.g., falling, walking, jumping) by analyzing skeleton sequences.
- Sports Analytics: Measuring golf swing planes or pitch mechanics.
- Virtual Try-on & AR: Fitting digital clothes onto human bodies.

cards.title: A digital protractor
cards.body: Imagine a therapist holding a physical plastic protractor against a patient's leg to measure a knee bend. Pose estimation acts like an automated digital protractor, measuring the angle between hip, knee, and ankle joints in every frame of a video.

cards.title: Elderly fall detection
cards.body: Smart home security cameras use pose estimation to detect falls. By analyzing the speed and direction of the skeletal center of mass, the system detects if a person goes from standing to lying down rapidly, triggering an alert.

cards.title: Not just for 2D
cards.body: You might think pose estimation is restricted to flat 2D videos. In reality, multi-camera setups or depth-predicting neural networks can reconstruct a complete **3D skeletal model** of a person, calculating deep spatial movement dynamics.

cards.title: Action Tracking
cards.body: How motion is analyzed using joint configurations:

left.text:
Hip-to-knee angle: 88°
Back aligned (no curvature)
Saves progress data

right.text:
Hip-to-knee angle: 120° (shallow)
Back rounded forward
Triggers real-time voice alert

exercises.prompt: How does a physical therapy app calculate a patient's knee-bend angle using pose estimation?

options.text: It calculates the angle between the lines connecting hip-to-knee and knee-to-ankle keypoints
It runs facial recognition on the patient's face
It crops the background and measures wall color changes
It estimates the user's heart rate from pixels

exercises.explain: Skeletal lines are vectors. We can calculate the angle of a joint using the vectors between keypoints.

exercises.prompt: True or False: fall-detection models using pose estimation analyze the movement of skeletal keypoints over time to identify rapid drops.
exercises.explain: True. Fall detection algorithms analyze the velocity and coordinates of skeletal keypoints over consecutive frames.

exercises.sentence: By using multiple cameras or depth estimators, models can reconstruct a ___ skeletal pose.
exercises.explain: Adding depth sensors or combining multiple camera angles enables the reconstruction of full 3D skeletal poses.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a direct application of pose estimation in retail?

options.text: A virtual fitting room app overlaying a digital dress onto a user's skeleton in real-time
Scanning barcodes to update warehouse inventory
Generating text descriptions of store layout

exercises.explain: Using joint tracking to map clothing assets to the user's skeletal dimensions is a core retail application.

exercises.prompt: You are designing an autonomous elderly home care camera. It must detect if a resident falls down, but it cannot upload raw video feeds to the cloud due to privacy regulations. How should you design this system?

options.text: Run the pose estimation model locally on the edge device, extract the anonymous skeletal coordinates, and upload only the coordinate sequences to the cloud for fall classification.
Upload raw 4K video feeds to a public server for remote human validation.
Disable the camera and use standard classification of ambient sounds.
Require the resident to wear a reflective motion capture suit at all times.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Running pose estimation locally allows you to discard the raw video immediately, sending only anonymous joint coordinates to the cloud. This ensures user privacy while maintaining full safety monitoring. Outstanding work! You have finished the Human Pose Estimation Basics unit. Next up: Optical Character Recognition (OCR)!
